challenges.prompt: Two siblings must share a counter. Following 'lifting state up', where should the useState live?
options: In App (their common parent), passed down as props
Duplicated in both Display and Controls
challenges.explanation: Lift the state to the closest common parent (App). App passes count to Display and a setter/callback to Controls, keeping one source of truth.

challenges.prompt: The child can't change the parent's state. What should the parent pass down?
options: Also pass a callback, e.g. <Toggle value={on} onToggle={() => setOn(o => !o)} />
challenges.explanation: Children change parent state by calling a callback the parent passes down. Give Toggle an onToggle prop wired to setOn; the child calls it, the parent updates, everything re-renders.

challenges.prompt: Order the steps to lift state up so two siblings can share it.
lines: Move the useState into the closest common parent
Pass the value down to each child as a prop
Pass a setter or callback down so a child can request a change
A child calls the callback, the parent updates state, both children re-render
challenges.explanation: Own the state in the common parent, send the value down, send a way to change it down, and let updates flow back up through the callback — a single source of truth.
